title: Public Law 114-94 (FAST Act)
content: Public Law 114-94 - Fixing America's Surface Transportation Act (FAST Act). Enacted December 4, 2015. This Act authorizes federal surface transportation programs for highways, highway safety, and transit for fiscal years 2016 through 2020. Section 1: Short Title. This Act may be cited as the Fixing America's Surface Transportation Act or the FAST Act. Section 2: Authorization Levels. Authorizes $305 billion over five years for highway, transit, highway safety, motor carrier safety, and rail programs. This represents a modest increase over previous authorization levels adjusted for inflation. Section 3: Highway Programs. Establishes funding levels and formulas for federal highway programs including the National Highway Performance Program (23 U.S.C. § 119), Surface Transportation Block Grant Program, Highway Safety Improvement Program, and Congestion Mitigation and Air Quality Program. Section 4: Transit Programs. Provides dedicated funding for public transportation capital investments, operating assistance for rural and small urban areas, and bus and bus facilities grants. Section 5: Highway Safety Programs. Continues funding for highway safety programs addressing impaired driving, occupant protection, traffic records, and motorcycle safety. Section 6: Motor Carrier Safety. Maintains funding for Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration programs to reduce crashes involving large trucks and buses. Section 7: Rail Programs. Provides limited funding for passenger rail improvements and grade crossing safety. Section 8: Performance Management and Accountability. Establishes performance-based approach to transportation decision-making, requiring states and metropolitan areas to set targets for safety, infrastructure condition, congestion, and emissions. This Act represented the first long-term surface transportation authorization in over a decade, providing states and local governments with funding certainty for planning major infrastructure projects.
